name: Black Bean Patties with Air-Fried Portobello Mushrooms
description: Smoky black bean patties paired with garlicky air-fried portobellos, layered in toasted buns. Makes 4–5 patties.

ingredients.name: tin black beans, drained and rinsed

steps.title: Soften the veg
steps.content: Heat a little of {0014} in a frying pan over medium heat. Sauté {0002} and {0004} for 5–6 minutes until softened. Add {0003} and cook for another minute. Set aside to cool slightly.

steps.title: Mash the beans
steps.content: Tip {0001} into a large bowl. Mash roughly two-thirds of them with a fork or potato masher, leaving the rest whole for texture.

steps.title: Mix the patty mixture
steps.content: Add the softened veg to the beans along with {0005}, {0006}, {0007}, {0008}, {0009}, {0010}, {0011}, {0012} and {0013}. Mix well until combined. The mixture should hold its shape when pressed — if it feels too wet, add another tablespoon of breadcrumbs.

steps.title: Shape and chill
steps.content: Divide the mixture into 4–5 patties, pressing firmly to compact them. Place on a plate, cover, and refrigerate for at least 20–30 minutes. This is important for helping them hold together during cooking.

steps.title: Prep the mushrooms
steps.content: Mix {0016}, {0017} and {0018} together. Brush over both sides of {0015}. Set aside until ready to cook.

steps.title: Air fry the patties
steps.content: Preheat your air fryer to 190°C. Brush both sides of the patties lightly with {0014}. Cook for 12–15 minutes, flipping carefully halfway through, until golden and firm on the outside.

steps.title: Air fry the mushrooms
steps.content: While the patties rest, cook the mushrooms at 190°C for 8–10 minutes, gill-side up for the first half, until tender and slightly caramelised.

steps.title: Assemble and serve
steps.content: Toast {0019} lightly. Layer with a patty, a Portobello mushroom, and any toppings you like — smashed avocado, sliced tomato, or shredded lettuce all work well.

notes: Leftover patties keep well in the fridge for 3 days and reheat in the air fryer at 180°C for 5–6 minutes. To freeze, shape the patties and freeze on a tray before cooking, then bag up once solid — they'll keep for up to 3 months.
